text: TIRUPATI DARSHAN INFORMATION === TYPES OF DARSHAN === 1. Dharma Darshan (Free Darshan) - Free of cost for all pilgrims - Waiting time: 4 to 12 hours depending on crowd - Our package facilitates Dharma Darshan with guide assistance - Available every day 2. Special Entry Darshan (SED) - Rs. 300 - Costs Rs. 300 per person - Waiting time: 1 to 4 hours - Available via TTD official portal: tirupatibalaji.ap.gov.in - Slots available 3 months in advance 3. VIP Break Darshan - For Indian nationals only - Not available under our package's tourist quota - Must be arranged through TTD portal directly - VIP darshan is NOT guaranteed and NOT part of our packages 4. Divya Darshan (Disabled/Senior) - Senior citizen priority is NOT available under our package - Separate TTD guidelines apply === DARSHAN TIMINGS === Temple opens: 2:30 AM Temple closes: 1:00 AM (next day) Darshan timings depend heavily on TTD crowd conditions Generally takes 1 to 5 hours from entry gate to darshan Our guide will assess the situation and guide accordingly === WHAT TO CARRY FOR DARSHAN === Valid government-issued photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Driving Licence, Passport, Voter ID) Comfortable footwear (removed at temple entrance) Devotional attire (traditional clothing recommended) No leather items allowed inside temple No mobile phones allowed in inner sanctum Children under 5 years - entry free, no darshan ticket needed Children under 1 year - free darshan allowed === TIRUMALA HILL INFORMATION === Tirumala is the hill town where the main temple is located Rooms at Tirumala are NOT permitted under our package as per TTD guidelines Pilgrims freshen up at base (Tirupati town) before going uphill Public restrooms available at Tirumala, guide will assist After Mundan (head tonsuring), freshen-up rooms not provided separately === CROWD & RUSH INFORMATION === Peak

temple is located Rooms at Tirumala are NOT permitted under our package as per TTD guidelines Pilgrims freshen up at base (Tirupati town) before going uphill Public restrooms available at Tirumala, guide will assist After Mundan (head tonsuring), freshen-up rooms not provided separately === CROWD & RUSH INFORMATION === Peak season: October to February (festive season) Brahmotsavam festival: extremely heavy rush (6-10 hour wait) Weekdays: generally less crowded than weekends Our guide monitors crowd and adjusts plan accordingly

TIRUPATI PACKAGE TOURS - FAQ & GENERAL INFORMATION === ABOUT THE COMPANY === Company: Tirupati Package Tours (operated by Sanchar6T) Website: tirupatipackagetours.com Phone 1: +91 8197882511 Phone 2: +91 9731312275 Email: enquiry@tirupatipackagetours.com WhatsApp: +91 8197882511 Office Hours: 9 AM to 9 PM all days === FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS === Q: Is it safe for single women or ladies travelling alone? A: Yes, absolutely safe. We have female guides and verified staff. Many solo women travel with us regularly. Safety is our top priority. Q: Can I book for a group? A: Yes, group bookings are welcome. Group discounts available for 10+ passengers. Contact us for group rates. Q: What documents do I need to carry? A: Carry a valid government photo ID - Aadhaar card, PAN card, Driving Licence, Passport, or Voter ID. One valid ID is mandatory for darshan entry. Q: Can I get VIP darshan through your package? A: No. VIP darshan is not part of our packages. Indians can book VIP darshan separately through the TTD official portal tirupatibalaji.ap.gov.in, usually 3 months in advance. We facilitate free Dharma Darshan. Q: Is food included in the package? A: Yes, 1 breakfast and 1 lunch in Package 1. 2 breakfasts and 2 lunches in Package 2. All food is pure vegetarian South Indian. Q: Can I get non-vegetarian food? A: No. Our packages only include pure vegetarian food. This is a religious pilgrimage, so only veg food is served. Q: What is the darshan waiting time? A: Depends on TTD crowd conditions. Generally 1 to 5 hours. On festival days it can be 6 to 10 hours. Our guide helps manage the process. Q: Can I upgrade to Special Entry Darshan (Rs. 300)? A: You can book SED separately via the TTD portal. Our package covers Dharma Darshan (free

TTD crowd conditions. Generally 1 to 5 hours. On festival days it can be 6 to 10 hours. Our guide helps manage the process. Q: Can I upgrade to Special Entry Darshan (Rs. 300)? A: You can book SED separately via the TTD portal. Our package covers Dharma Darshan (free darshan). You can use your own SED ticket if booked. Q: Are children charged? A: Children under 1 year travel free (lap seat). Children under 5 years get 50% discount. 5 years and above pay full fare. Q: Is there AC in the bus? A: Yes, all our buses are AC. Q: Is there a sleeper bus option? A: Yes, both seater and sleeper AC bus options are available. Choose during booking. Q: Can I book a private cab instead of bus? A: Yes, cab arrangements available on request. Call +91 8197882511. Q: What happens if I miss the bus? A: Contact us immediately on +91 8197882511. We will try our best to arrange alternate transport but cannot guarantee it. Please arrive at boarding point 15 minutes early. Q: Is the trip customizable? A: Yes! We can create custom itineraries. Call +91 8197882511 or email enquiry@tirupatipackagetours.com. Q: Can I get a room at Tirumala (the hilltop)? A: No. Rooms at Tirumala are NOT part of our package per TTD guidelines. Accommodation is arranged in Tirupati town. TTD manages their own rooms separately. Q: Is there hot water in rooms? A: Yes, 24/7 hot water (geyser) available. Q: What about Mundan (head shaving)? A: After Mundan, Tirumala rooms are not provided. Public restrooms are available, and our guide will assist you. Q: Do you cover Srikalahasti? A: Yes, in Package 2 (2 Days 2 Nights). Srikalahasti temple visit is included. Rahu-Ketu Pooja can be done at your own cost. Q: How

(head shaving)? A: After Mundan, Tirumala rooms are not provided. Public restrooms are available, and our guide will assist you. Q: Do you cover Srikalahasti? A: Yes, in Package 2 (2 Days 2 Nights). Srikalahasti temple visit is included. Rahu-Ketu Pooja can be done at your own cost. Q: How do I cancel my booking? A: Call +91 8197882511 as early as possible. Cancellation policy applies based on how close the travel date is. Q: Can I get a receipt or invoice for my booking? A: Yes, booking confirmation and e-ticket are sent to your registered email after payment. Q: How far in advance should I book? A: At least 3 to 7 days in advance is recommended, especially for weekends and festival seasons when seats fill quickly. Q: What is the refund policy? A: Refunds depend on the date of cancellation. Contact +91 8197882511 for details. === BEST TIME TO VISIT TIRUPATI === September to February → Best time to visit (pleasant weather) March to June → Very hot, not recommended July to August → Moderate rain, less crowd === DARSHAN TYPES (TEMPLE VISIT OPTIONS) === Sarva Darshan (Free Darshan) → Free entry, long waiting time Sheeghra Darshan (Rs. 300) → Faster entry, paid Divya Darshan → For devotees coming by footpath VIP Break Darshan → For special category or high donations === SPECIAL SEVAS (RITUALS) === Suprabhatam → Early morning prayer Thomala Seva Archana Kalyanotsavam All special seva tickets must be booked in advance (very limited availability) === IMPORTANT FESTIVALS (HEAVY CROWD DAYS) === Brahmotsavam → Most important festival, very heavy crowd Vaikunta Ekadasi → Very heavy crowd Rathasapthami New Year and major holidays → Expect long waiting times === SRIKALAHASTI TEMPLE === Location: About 36 km from Tirupati Famous for: Rahu-Ketu Pooja (removes planetary doshas) Deity: Lord

IMPORTANT FESTIVALS (HEAVY CROWD DAYS) === Brahmotsavam → Most important festival, very heavy crowd Vaikunta Ekadasi → Very heavy crowd Rathasapthami New Year and major holidays → Expect long waiting times === SRIKALAHASTI TEMPLE === Location: About 36 km from Tirupati Famous for: Rahu-Ketu Pooja (removes planetary doshas) Deity: Lord Shiva (Srikalahasteeswara) Unique feature: One of the Pancha Bhuta Stalas (Air Linga) Included in: Package 2 only === TIRUPATI TOWN INFORMATION === Tirupati is a city in Andhra Pradesh, South India Famous as one of the most visited religious destinations in the world Lord Venkateswara Temple is located in Tirumala, about 20-25 km from Tirupati city Nearest airport: Tirupati Airport Nearest railway station: Tirupati Railway Station Our buses drop and pick up from designated points in the city
